hid.description: Canonical USB HID usage identity used as the cross-platform anchor for the key inventory.

linux-evdev.description: Linux input-event keycode layer using KEY_* constants from the kernel input subsystem.

linux-xkb.description: Linux XKB keycode-name layer used to label physical key positions above evdev.

linux-xkb-keysym.description: Linux XKB keysym layer used for symbolic key meaning after XKB translation.

windows-scancode.description: Windows scan-code layer for physical key identity as delivered through keyboard input messages.

windows-virtual.description: Windows virtual-key and app-command layer for logical key and command identity.

mac.description: macOS native keyboard identity across Carbon virtual keycodes, AppKit function characters, media events, and system-defined subtypes.

web.description: Web UI Events key/code layer used by browsers for standardized DOM keyboard identity.

qmk.description: QMK firmware keycode vocabulary for programmable keyboards, covering standard keyboard keys, modifiers, layers, consumer controls, and many firmware-level pseudo-keys.

kmonad.description: KMonad key-name vocabulary used by the KMonad remapper configuration language for cross-platform keyboard remapping and advanced key behaviors.

kanata.description: Kanata key-name vocabulary used by the Kanata remapper configuration language for cross-platform key remapping, layers, and tap-hold style behaviors.

keyd.description: keyd key-name vocabulary used by the Linux keyd remapper for system-wide evdev/uinput-based remapping, layers, and firmware-like keyboard behaviors.

karabiner-elements.description: Karabiner-Elements key vocabulary for macOS remapping, covering ordinary keys, modifiers, consumer keys, system keys, and Apple-specific vendor usages.

autohotkey.description: AutoHotkey named-key vocabulary for Windows keyboard automation and remapping, covering standard keys, modifiers, multimedia keys, and many Windows-specific logical key names.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux hid-input folds HID keyboard usages 0x31 and 0x32 onto the same KEY_BACKSLASH evdev keycode.

linux-xkb.comment: Because the Linux kernel maps this HID usage to KEY_BACKSLASH, XKB also sees it through the same BKSL key path.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: Linux reaches this key through the same KEY_BACKSLASH path, so the XKB symbolic result collapses onto the same backslash meaning.

web.comment: UI Events uses the same Backslash code for both HID 0x31 and HID 0x32 on standard 102-key layouts because many platforms do not distinguish them at the scancode layer.

kmonad.comment: KMonad currently uses the same backslash key name for this physical key rather than a distinct non-US-pound token.

iso-#.comment: Distinct HID key for the ISO/UK-style #~ key partially tucked under Enter. It is different from the ISO key next to left Shift (HID 0x64), but many software stacks collapse it onto backslash-like semantics.

intl-ro.comment: JIS KEY_RO is primarily a printable key. It has no distinct standard XKB keysym and often produces the printable backslash symbol, so the physical-key-specific mapping is left missing here.

windows-virtual.comment: Windows exposes keypad Enter through the generic Return virtual key rather than a distinct keypad-enter virtual key.

mac.name: NSInsertFunctionKey
mac.comment: AppKit distinguishes Insert via NSInsertFunctionKey. In the virtual-keycode API, both Insert and Help collapse onto kVK_Help.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: xkbcommon-keysyms.h defines numbered macro keysyms like XF86Macro1..XF86Macro30, but no direct keysym for KEY_MACRO

kp-sign-change.comment: Calculator-style keypad sign toggle: changes the current numeric entry between positive and negative rather than acting as an arithmetic operator.

pause.comment: Classic keyboard Pause key. On Windows, the special Pause scan code is emitted for this key, while Break is emitted for Ctrl+Pause. GNOME treats the Pause key itself as a media-pause trigger and sends the MPRIS "Pause" action.

autohotkey.name: Pause
autohotkey.comment: AutoHotkey also recognizes CtrlBreak for the classic Windows Ctrl+Pause behavior on this key.

hanja.comment: HID LANG2 is dual-use across keyboard ecosystems: Windows and Linux usually expose it as the Korean Hanja conversion key, while Apple Japanese keyboards use the same HID usage for the Eisu key.

windows-virtual.comment: Windows distinguishes this key at the scan-code level, but the available JP layout evidence did not establish a distinct canonical virtual key for IntlYen.

possibleAlternatives.name: Stop
possibleAlternatives.comment: Keyboard-page Stop is a separate HID usage, but Linux does not map it to KEY_STOP here.

linux-xkb-keysym.name: XF86Stop
linux-xkb-keysym.comment: Standard XKB stop/abort semantics also collapse onto Cancel, even though stop keeps XF86Stop as its canonical dedicated keysym here.

stop.comment: Linux KEY_STOP / XKB STOP come from Consumer AC Stop. HID Keyboard/Keypad Stop remains only an informational alternative because Linux does not map it to this keycode.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: XKB reuses XF86Send for both KEY_SEND and KEY_SENDFILE, so send-file keeps this only as an informational alternative.
xkbcommon-keysyms.h defines generic delete keysyms like Delete and KP_Delete, but no dedicated keysym for KEY_DELETEFILE

www.comment: GNOME treats this as the web-browser launcher key and opens an http: action.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: XKB reuses XF86Favorites for both KEY_FAVORITES and KEY_BOOKMARKS, so bookmarks keeps this only as an informational alternative.

bookmarks.comment: Bookmarks/favorites key. Use it for bookmark or saved-item lists rather than browser history.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: xkbcommon-keysyms.h notes: Currently no use case for CD-specific keysyms

media-next.comment: GNOME treats this as the media next-track key and sends the MPRIS "Next" action.

media-prev.comment: GNOME treats this as the media previous-track key and sends the MPRIS "Previous" action.

media-rewind.comment: GNOME treats this as the media rewind key and sends the MPRIS "Rewind" action.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: xkbcommon-keysyms.h states TODO: unclear function _EVDEVK(0x0aa) KEY_ISO

xf86close.name: XF86Close
xf86close.comment: xkeyboard-config symbols-inet maps KEY_EXIT to XF86Close even though xkbcommon-keysyms.h defines XF86Exit specifically for KEY_EXIT.

exit.comment: Exit / quit key. The Linux kernel also maps HID Generic Desktop System Menu Exit (0x01/0x88) and Consumer AC Exit (0x0c/0x204) to the same KEY_EXIT / XKB key. XKB additionally has an active xkeyboard-config mapping to XF86Close, but xkbcommon-keysyms.h defines XF86Exit specifically for KEY_EXIT, so the other mapping is preserved here only for reference.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: xkbcommon-keysyms.h states TODO: unclear function _EVDEVK(0x0af) KEY_MOVE
xkbcommon-keysyms.h states TODO: unclear function _EVDEVK(0x0b0) KEY_EDIT

media-fast-forward.comment: GNOME treats this as the media fast-forward key and sends the MPRIS "FastForward" action.

display-brightness.name: Display Brightness
display-brightness.comment: the kernel maps both HID Consumer 0x65 and 0x71 to KEY_CAMERA

email.comment: Linux exposes KEY_EMAIL separately from KEY_MAIL, and the kernel maps HID Consumer 0x80 to it even though the HID tables name that usage Selection. XKB and Windows flatten the launcher behavior to the generic mail-launch action, so those canonical mappings live under mail instead.

assign.name: Assign
assign.comment: the kernel maps both HID Consumer 0x199 and 0x81 to KEY_CHAT

market-monitor-or-finance-browser.name: AL Market Monitor or Finance Browser

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: xkbcommon-keysyms.h states TODO: unclear function _EVDEVK(0x0de) KEY_ALTERASE

media.comment: GNOME treats this as the generic media launcher key rather than a transport-control key.

battery.comment: GNOME treats this as the battery-status key and shows battery information/OSD.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth radio key. GNOME has a dedicated Bluetooth airplane-mode path for Bluetooth-specific rfkill keys; use this for toggling the Bluetooth radio rather than general media control.

wlan.comment: Wireless LAN radio key. Use this for toggling Wi‑Fi/radio state on devices that expose a dedicated WLAN hardware key.

uwb.comment: Ultra‑Wideband radio key. This is a dedicated wireless-radio hardware key for UWB-capable devices rather than a normal application/media shortcut.

windows-virtual.comment: Windows exposes only the generic media next-track virtual key here, not a distinct next-video-source virtual key.

video-next.comment: Next video-source key. The kernel meaning is “drive next video source”, so this is for cycling display/video inputs and is distinct from next-track media playback.

windows-virtual.comment: Windows exposes only the generic media previous-track virtual key here, not a distinct previous-video-source virtual key.

brightness-auto.comment: Kernel meaning: enable automatic brightness control. Linux aliases KEY_BRIGHTNESS_ZERO to the same keycode, but the kernel comment defines the primary meaning as automatic brightness.

display-off.comment: Display power key. The kernel meaning is “put the display device into the off state”, so this is for turning the display panel off rather than changing brightness.

wwan.comment: Wireless Wide Area Network radio key. The kernel explicitly defines this for LTE/UMTS/GSM-style mobile broadband radios; `wimax` is just an alias of the same Linux keycode.

ok.comment: Confirm/accept key, typically the OK/Enter button in a remote-control or D-pad style navigation cluster.

goto.comment: Direct navigation key. On media/remotes this is typically used to jump directly to a channel, title, time, or indexed location.

clear.comment: Clear-entry key. Use it for clearing the current value, typed digits, or current selection rather than general delete/backspace behavior.

sysrq-attention.comment: Legacy extended keyboard control key. Historically distinct from Print Screen even when some PC keyboards colocated the two labels.

prior.comment: Legacy extended keyboard control key on the HID keyboard page.

legacy-return.comment: Legacy extended keyboard control key, distinct from the normal Return/Enter key and keypad Enter.

separator.comment: Legacy extended keyboard control key on the HID keyboard page.

out.comment: Legacy extended keyboard control key on the HID keyboard page.

oper.comment: Legacy extended keyboard control key on the HID keyboard page.

power2.comment: Secondary power key. systemd-logind handles KEY_POWER2 through the same power-button path as KEY_POWER, and also supports a distinct long-press action.

option.comment: Options/menu-options key. Treat it as a device or application options command rather than the keyboard Alt/Option modifier.

time.comment: Time-display key. On consumer/media devices this is typically used to show or enter a time/timecode/clock-style view rather than general scheduling logic.

vendor.comment: Vendor-specific consumer key. This is reserved for OEM-defined behavior, so its exact action depends on the device vendor rather than a shared desktop convention.

archive.comment: Archive key. Use it for archive/recording-library style actions such as opening archived media or stored items.

channel.comment: Channel-selection key. Use it for changing or entering channels in tuner/set-top-box style interfaces.

favorites.comment: Favorites/bookmarks key. Use it for opening or cycling a favorites list rather than generic “like” behavior.

epg.comment: Electronic Program Guide key. This is the standard TV/set-top-box guide/listings concept.

pvr.comment: Personal Video Recorder key. The Linux kernel comment maps this to “Media Select Home”, so it is typically the DVR/PVR home/library key.

language.comment: Language-selection key. On media devices this is typically used to switch audio language or similar playback language settings.

subtitle.comment: Subtitle / closed-caption key. KMonad also aliases this as `caption`, which matches the media-device closed-caption meaning.

aspect-ratio.comment: Aspect-ratio switching key for display/video devices.

pc.comment: Source-selection key for switching to a PC/computer input on a display or media device.

tv.comment: Source-selection key for switching to a TV tuner/input mode.

tv2.comment: Alternate TV/cable source-selection key. KMonad aliases it as `cable`, which fits that secondary TV-input meaning.

vcr.comment: Source-selection key for switching to a VCR/video-recorder input or mode.

vcr2.comment: Alternate VCR/video-recorder source key. KMonad aliases it as `vcr+`, suggesting a secondary VCR input or related recorder mode.

hid.name: AL Audio or Video Capture or Playback

player.comment: Media-player source/application key for jumping to a playback device or player mode rather than transport control.

dvd.comment: Source-selection key for switching to DVD playback/input mode.

next.comment: GNOME treats this as the media next-track key and sends the MPRIS "Next" action.

restart.comment: Restart / reset key. The Linux kernel also maps HID Consumer Restart (0x0c/0x0f) and Generic Desktop System Warm Restart (0x01/0x8f) to the same KEY_RESTART / XKB key, so those alternatives are preserved here for reference.

break.comment: Linux KEY_BREAK is a separate modern input key that maps to XF86Break. It is not the same source as the classic Pause/Break keyboard behavior, where Break is produced as Ctrl+Pause on the Pause key.

previous.comment: GNOME treats this as the media previous-track key and sends the MPRIS "Previous" action.

mac.comment: macOS exposes System Context Menu through the same native kVK_ContextualMenu key as Keyboard Application, so mappy keeps menu as the canonical Mac key identity.

media-repeat.comment: GNOME treats this as the media repeat key and sends the MPRIS "Repeat" action.

hid.name: AL Log or Journal or Timecard

screensaver.comment: GNOME treats this as the screensaver/lock key and locks the session.

hid.comment: USB HID page 0x00 is Undefined; no standard usage definition is published for this usage.
USB HID page 0x00 is Undefined; no standard usage definition is published for this usage.

extend-selection.comment: Legacy extended editing key. ExSel stands for Extend Selection.

hid.comment: USB HID page 0x00 is Undefined; no standard usage definition is published for this usage.
USB HID page 0x00 is Undefined; no standard usage definition is published for this usage.
USB HID page 0x00 is Undefined; no standard usage definition is published for this usage.

suspend.comment: System suspend key. systemd-logind treats KEY_SUSPEND as suspend-to-disk / hibernate; KDE binds Qt::Key_Hibernate as “Hibernate”. systemd also supports a distinct long-press action.

hid.comment: USB HID page 0x00 is Undefined; no standard usage definition is published for this usage.

homepage.comment: GNOME treats this as the home launcher key and invokes its home action.

search.comment: GNOME treats this as the search launcher key and invokes its search action.

windows-virtual.comment: Windows collapses dedicated Katakana input into the generic IME Kana mode virtual key, so katakana keeps this only as an informational alternative.

calc.comment: GNOME treats this as the calculator launcher key and opens GNOME Calculator.

front.comment: Legacy front-page/front-panel style key from the Linux input namespace. KMonad avoids the alias `select` here because it conflicts with KEY_SELECT, so keep it distinct from the generic select/menu-pick key.

keyboard-illumination-toggle.comment: Keyboard backlight toggle key. GNOME toggles the keyboard-brightness service; KDE binds it as “Toggle Keyboard Backlight”, shows an OSD, and restores keyboard brightness after resume.

keyboard-illumination-up.comment: Keyboard backlight increase key. GNOME steps the keyboard-brightness service up; KDE increases keyboard brightness, shows an OSD, and restores keyboard brightness after resume.

keyboard-illumination-down.comment: Keyboard backlight decrease key. GNOME steps the keyboard-brightness service down; KDE decreases keyboard brightness, shows an OSD, and restores keyboard brightness after resume.

mute-mic.comment: GNOME binds this as the microphone mute key and routes it through the quiet sound-action path for input-device mute, rather than the normal output-volume mute action.

select.comment: Select / menu-pick key. The Linux kernel also maps HID Consumer Menu (0x0c/0x09) and Generic Desktop System Menu Select (0x01/0x89) to the same KEY_SELECT / XKB key, so those alternatives are preserved here for reference. Distinct from KEY_FRONT.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: XKB reuses Redo for both KEY_REDO and the keyboard-page Again key, so again keeps this only as an informational alternative.

mac.name: NSHelpFunctionKey
mac.comment: AppKit distinguishes Help via NSHelpFunctionKey. In the virtual-keycode API, both Help and Insert collapse onto kVK_Help.

sleep.comment: System sleep key. systemd-logind treats KEY_SLEEP as suspend-to-RAM; KDE binds Qt::Key_Sleep as “Suspend”. Both allow configured system behavior, and systemd also supports a distinct long-press action.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: XKB commonly reuses Print for KEY_PRINT, but that collapses it with Print Screen / SysRq rather than preserving the distinct consumer print-command semantics.

windows-virtual.comment: Windows exposes Print Screen here, not a distinct consumer print-command virtual key.

mac.comment: macOS AppKit exposes application-level print through NSPrintFunctionKey, but mappy keeps print as the canonical Mac key identity for the shared Print function-character.

print-command.comment: Consumer-key print command. This is distinct from Print Screen / SysRq: use it for an application-level print action rather than screen capture.

control-panel.comment: Settings/control-panel key. GNOME does not bind this exact symbol directly, but it does have a dedicated control-center launcher action for settings-style keys.

linux-evdev.name: KEY_EJECTCLOSECD
linux-evdev.comment: Linux also defines a combined eject/close optical-drive key, but mappy treats it only as an informational variant of eject-cd.

eject-cd.comment: Optical-media eject key for opening the tray / ejecting the disc.

display-toggle.comment: Display/input switching key. Use it for toggling display outputs or cycling video sources, matching the web `DisplayToggleIntExt` meaning.

task-select.comment: Task/application switcher key. The web name `SelectTask` suggests the operating-system task switcher or application-selection surface.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: No dedicated standard XKB keysym for KEY_STOPCD; XF86AudioStop is only a generic media-stop approximation rather than a 1:1 equivalent.

cd-stop.comment: Optical-media stop key. Distinct from a generic media stop key when devices expose a dedicated CD playback command.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: No dedicated standard XKB keysym for KEY_PLAYCD; XF86AudioPlay is only a generic media-play approximation rather than a 1:1 equivalent.

cd-play.comment: Optical-media play key. Distinct from the generic media play key when devices expose a dedicated CD playback command.

guide.comment: Program-guide key. The Linux kernel comment identifies this as “Media Select Program Guide”, so it is the guide/listings key.

zoom.comment: Zoom/view-toggle key. Linux aliases KEY_ZOOM to KEY_FULL_SCREEN, and the web mapping names it ZoomToggle.

airplane-mode.comment: Global radio toggle / airplane-mode key. GNOME toggles airplane mode for it, and the kernel describes it as the key that controls all radios.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux treats Apple Dashboard as the same evdev key identity used for All Applications. KEY_DASHBOARD is an alias of KEY_ALL_APPLICATIONS.

linux-xkb.comment: Linux maps the collapsed Dashboard/All Applications evdev identity to the same XKB key as all-applications.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: Linux exposes Dashboard through the same XKB keysym used for all-applications.

apple-dashboard.comment: Apple Dashboard key on the Apple vendor keyboard page.

apple-show-desktop.comment: Apple Show Desktop / Expose Desktop key on the Apple vendor keyboard page.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux maps Apple Video Mirror to the same evdev key identity used for display-toggle.

linux-xkb.comment: Linux exposes Apple Video Mirror through the same XKB key as display-toggle.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: Linux exposes Apple Video Mirror through the same XKB keysym used for display-toggle.

apple-video-mirror.comment: Apple video-mirroring key on the Apple vendor top-case page.

hid.name: AL Command Line Processor or Run

terminal-or-run.comment: Application-launch HID key for a terminal or run-command interface.

hid.name: AL Preemptive Halt Task or Application
AL Remote Networking or ISP Connect
AL Smart Card Information or Help

kp-equals-as400.comment: Distinct from standard keypad equals; HID defines a separate keypad-equals usage for AS/400 keyboards.

bass-down.comment: Consumer audio-control key for lowering bass.

bass-up.comment: Consumer audio-control key for raising bass.

locking-caps-lock.comment: Legacy mechanical-locking Caps Lock usage, distinct from ordinary Caps Lock.

locking-num-lock.comment: Legacy mechanical-locking Num Lock usage, distinct from ordinary Num Lock.

locking-scroll-lock.comment: Legacy mechanical-locking Scroll Lock usage, distinct from ordinary Scroll Lock.

loudness.comment: Consumer audio-control key for loudness mode.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x40 to KEY_MENU, the same evdev key used for Keyboard Application.

menu-consumer.comment: Consumer-page menu key, distinct from keyboard-menu usages.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x43 to KEY_DOWN.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x46 to KEY_ESC.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x44 to KEY_LEFT.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x45 to KEY_RIGHT.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x42 to KEY_UP.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x48 to KEY_KPMINUS.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x47 to KEY_KPPLUS.

picture-in-picture-swap.comment: Consumer display-control key for picture-in-picture source swapping.

picture-in-picture-toggle.comment: Consumer display-control key for picture-in-picture mode.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux defines KEY_3D_MODE, but hid-input does not map HID Consumer 0x6e to that evdev key.
Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x1a6 to KEY_HELP.

help-center.comment: Application-launch HID key for an integrated help center.

linux-evdev.comment: Linux hid-input maps HID Consumer 0x1bd to KEY_INFO.

oem-feature-browser.comment: Application-launch HID key for an OEM feature browser.

linux-evdev.comment: the kernel maps HID Consumer 0x0004 to KEY_BRIGHTNESSUP

linux-xkb.comment: Linux exposes this through the same XKB key as brightness-up after the kernel fold.

linux-xkb-keysym.comment: Linux exposes this through the same XKB keysym as brightness-up after the kernel fold.

microphone.comment: Consumer microphone-control key. Linux folds this usage into brightness-up, but that is not a semantic match.

international7.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These International7+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.

international8.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These International7+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.

international9.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These International7+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.

lang6.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These Lang6+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.

lang7.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These Lang6+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.

lang8.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These Lang6+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.

lang9.comment: Region- or vendor-specific keyboard usage. These Lang6+ keys are part of the HID keyboard page but are rarely present on actual modern keyboards.
